title: homalobus grallator
side_effects: Homalobus grallator can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as interact with certain medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can lead to liver and kidney damage, and in severe cases, it can cause seizures and coma. If not handled correctly, Homalobus grallator can also trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction.
